response.reasoning: Step 1 — Experience depth: The candidate has been coding professionally since 2009 (15+ years) with continuous full-stack roles. The 6-year minimum is easily exceeded. Step 2 — Primary stack alignment: TypeScript and Node.js appear across multiple roles (re:cinq, Optilyz, RIDE Capital, WatchUp). React is confirmed at Optilyz. Vitest is absent by name, but the candidate authored D-language testing frameworks and explicitly practiced TDD across roles, indicating strong testing culture. NestJS is never mentioned — a notable gap given it is a named primary technology. Step 3 — Secondary stack: Java appears in skills and at Altom (robotic arms, orchestration tool), but Kotlin is absent entirely. Step 4 — Infrastructure and databases: MongoDB confirmed at Optilyz, MySQL and SQL listed in skills. AWS CDK confirmed at Optilyz. Docker and Kubernetes confirmed at GISCollective and Altom. Step 5 — AI agent experience: Current role at re:cinq explicitly names AI-agent infrastructure and MCP (Model Context Protocol) tooling — a direct hit on the job's AI agent requirement. Step 6 — Seniority signals: Staff-level title at two employers, conference talks, open-source framework authorship, and mentoring/workshop delivery all point to senior IC operating above the role's expectations. Step 7 — Score decision: High overlap on core stack, AI agent work, testing maturity, and cloud/infra. Two gaps (NestJS, Kotlin) are learnable and not disqualifying at senior level.
strengths: 15+ years of progressive full-stack experience with TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, and AWS — direct match to the primary stack
Active AI-agent and MCP tooling work in current role at re:cinq — directly satisfies the AI agent development requirement
Deep testing culture: authored open-source TDD frameworks, ran TDD workshops, and applied TDD/pairing/trunk-based dev in production environments
concerns: NestJS is never mentioned anywhere in the resume — a named primary framework the team actively uses
Kotlin is absent from the skill set; only Java is listed, leaving the secondary backend language requirement partially unmet
Vitest is not explicitly named; testing experience is strong but focused on custom and Jest-era tooling rather than the team's current framework

key_factors.factor: TypeScript/Node.js/React depth across multiple production roles

response.justification: The candidate is a strong match for this role. Their TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB, AWS, Docker, and Kubernetes experience aligns well with the primary stack, and their current work on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling is a precise fit for the AI agent development requirement. The NestJS gap is real but not disqualifying at this seniority level — a developer who has architected micro-services at scale and built their own testing frameworks will onboard to a framework quickly. The Kotlin absence is a minor concern given Kotlin is secondary. A screening interview to probe NestJS familiarity and Vitest adoption is the right next step.
